product: New Relic change tracking (deployment change markers) via NerdGraph

transport.rule: one GraphQL document per request; all dynamic values travel in variables; the user API key never appears in URLs, documents, or exception text

mutation.name: changeTrackingCreateEvent

input_shape.omit_rule: absent optional fields are omitted entirely, never sent as null

mutation.change_tracking_id_rule: changeTrackingId is always generated by New Relic; the client can never choose it
mutation.flags_rule: deployment/basic is a standard kind: ALLOW_CUSTOM_CATEGORY_OR_TYPE must not be sent; FAIL_ON_FIELD_LENGTH is sent so oversized fields fail loudly instead of being silently truncated
mutation.timestamp_rule: timestamps are epoch milliseconds and must be within +/- 24 hours of the current time or the payload is rejected; the client validates overrides locally before any request

rules: every marker carries its caller-supplied stable change identifier as customAttributes.change_id
a changeId already present in the injected ledger is replayed from the ledger without any HTTP request

transient failures (HTTP 429 and 5xx) are retried with byte-identical request bodies - the timestamp is computed once per record() call so a duplicate produced by an ambiguous retry is exactly identifiable by change_id and timestamp
mutation-level GraphQL errors are permanent validation failures and are never retried
the ledger records changeId -> changeTrackingId only after a confirmed success

entity_search.single_match_rule: exactly one matching entity is required; zero matches and multiple matches (including a non-null nextCursor) are distinct errors

validation_error.message: Invalid category and type: only documented kinds are accepted without ALLOW_CUSTOM_CATEGORY_OR_TYPE
